title: Reading and Use of English – Part 2
description: For questions 9–16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only ONE word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: The woolly mammoth was a majestic creature that roamed the frozen steppes (0) during the last Ice Age, eventually becoming extinct thousands of years ago. (9) Although most mammoths died out around ten thousand years ago, a small population survived on a remote island until much later. (10) Such was the isolation of Wrangel Island that these creatures remained there long after their mainland relatives had vanished. (11) Provided that the climate stayed stable, they might have continued to thrive in their icy sanctuary for even longer. (12) Not only did the mammoths face a changing environment, but they also suffered from a significant loss of genetic diversity. (13) Regardless of the specific cause, their final disappearance marked the end of an era for Earth's megafauna. (14) Not until recently have scientists successfully sequenced the mammoth's entire genome from well-preserved remains found in the Siberian permafrost. (15) In spite of the technical challenges, some researchers are now exploring the possibility of bringing this species back through cloning. (16) Whether or not such a feat is ethically or practically possible remains a subject of fierce debate within the scientific community. These ancient remains provide a window into a world that was lost.

example.text: The woolly mammoth was a majestic creature that roamed the frozen steppes (0) during the last Ice Age.
example.explanation: Preposition used to indicate a period of time.

questions.explanation: Conjunction used to introduce a contrast or concession.
Determiner used for emphasis at the start of the clause.
Part of the 'Not only... but also' correlative conjunction.
Part of the fixed phrase 'Regardless of'.
Part of the 'Not until' structure requiring inversion ('have scientists successfully sequenced').
Part of the prepositional linker 'In spite of'.
Conjunction introducing two possibilities or a doubt.
